{"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,5,4]],"date-time":"2026-05-04T14:05:24Z","timestamp":1777903524507,"version":"3.51.4"},"reference-count":17,"publisher":"Ovid Technologies (Wolters Kluwer Health)","issue":"3","license":[{"start":{"date-parts":[[2026,4,30]],"date-time":"2026-04-30T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1777507200000},"content-version":"unspecified","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0"}],"content-domain":{"domain":["lww.com","ovid.com"],"crossmark-restriction":true},"short-container-title":[],"published-print":{"date-parts":[[2026,5]]},"abstract":"<jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Background:</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>Oral health is critical to overall well-being, with abnormalities leading to various health issues. Pemphigus is an autoimmune disease characterized by painful blisters on the skin and mucous membranes, necessitating accurate diagnosis and timely treatment.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Objective:</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>This study aims to develop an artificial intelligence (AI) tool utilizing deep learning techniques for the detection of pemphigus from clinical and histological images, enhancing diagnostic accuracy.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Methods:</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>A supervised learning approach was employed, utilizing a pre-trained MobileNet deep learning model to classify histology images into healthy and pemphigus categories. The dataset was prepared through cropping, resizing, and labeling of images.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Results:</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>The MobileNet model achieved an accuracy of over 92% in distinguishing between normal mucosa and pemphigus lesions. The proposed AI tool can serve as an initial diagnostic check, facilitating faster treatment planning.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>
                  <jats:sec>
                    <jats:title>Conclusion:</jats:title>
                    <jats:p>The implementation of an AI-based diagnostic tool for pemphigus detection demonstrates significant potential in reducing the diagnostic burden in clinical settings. Future work will focus on real-time disease detection applications.</jats:p>
                  </jats:sec>","DOI":"10.4103/ijd.ijd_341_25","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,1,22]],"date-time":"2026-01-22T07:01:57Z","timestamp":1769065317000},"page":"199-203","update-policy":"https://doi.org/10.1097/lww.0000000000001000","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Exploring AI as a Diagnostic Tool in Medical Imaging for Dermatopathological Diseases"],"prefix":"10.4103","volume":"71","author":[{"given":"Pravallika","family":"Kakada","sequence":"first","affiliation":[{"name":"From the Department of Oral Pathology and Microbiology, Saveetha Dental College and Hospitals, Saveetha Institute of Medical and Technical Sciences, Saveetha University, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Pratibha","family":"Ramani","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[{"name":"From the Department of Oral Pathology and Microbiology, Saveetha Dental College and Hospitals, Saveetha Institute of Medical and Technical Sciences, Saveetha University, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"Venkatesan","family":"Rajinikanth","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[{"name":"Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Division of Research and Innovation, Saveetha School of Engineering, SIMATS, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"given":"V.
